title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers messaging outcomes, delivery claims, and evidence into a new messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 3797 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Evidence Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Evidence Contract, keep Outcome Evidence Contract connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

Competency probe: Plan browser models, TypeScript compilation, contract tests, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, and production transfer gates.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Transfer Evidence Plan condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Transfer Evidence Plan output and hide the violated messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Transfer Evidence Plan.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Transfer Evidence Plan without its stated preconditions, omit the messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Transfer Evidence Plan for messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter.
